course.title: Independent Product Studio II: Multi-Actor Systems, Delivery, and Operations
course.description: Learners who have independently shipped and defended one product and are ready to lead a more consequential multi-actor system through collaboration, distributed failure, trusted delivery, operations, migration, and longitudinal validation. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate a second independent product cycle that begins with maintenance reality and advances through multi-actor service design, co-design, workflow state, architecture, data lifecycle, identity, authorization, resilience, governance, operations, capacity, trusted delivery, migration, recovery, and longitudinal evidence through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate explicit collaboration and personal-contribution evidence plus five different team and operations milestones through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate changed-context tasks that require stronger system, human, and operating evidence than the first studio through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves the prior problem, stakeholder, affected-user, accessibility, security, privacy, evidence, revision, test, operation, recovery, contribution, and non-claim boundaries before adding complexity.

Browser labs analyze deterministic original evidence manifests only. They never execute learner shell commands, contact research participants or external services, deploy software, access secrets, or claim live accessibility, security, privacy, load, recovery, legal, or production behavior.

Passing work requires a prediction, traceable artifact, deterministic verification, changed and rejected cases, causal diagnosis, repair evidence, affected-user or stakeholder evidence where appropriate, explicit limitations, and an accountable owner.

competencies.statement: Audit which prior claims survived real use, changed data, dependency drift, support, accessibility, security, and recovery conditions.
misconceptions: A product that shipped remains proven until code changes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Audit which prior claims survived real use, changed data, dependency drift, support, accessibility, security, and recovery conditions.

competencies.statement: Classify feedback by user outcome, evidence source, frequency, severity, reach, confidence, and strategic fit.
misconceptions: The loudest request deserves the highest priority.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify feedback by user outcome, evidence source, frequency, severity, reach, confidence, and strategic fit.

competencies.statement: Measure dependency, defect, support, content, data, infrastructure, and cognitive maintenance cost before adding scope.
misconceptions: Feature development is the only meaningful project cost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure dependency, defect, support, content, data, infrastructure, and cognitive maintenance cost before adding scope.

competencies.statement: Compare repair, simplify, extend, replace, archive, and retire options against outcomes and total risk.
misconceptions: Starting a new version is always easier than repairing the old one.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare repair, simplify, extend, replace, archive, and retire options against outcomes and total risk.

competencies.statement: Define which unfamiliar multi-actor, distributed, operational, or leadership competencies the second project must independently prove.
misconceptions: A larger codebase automatically demonstrates more advanced mastery.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define which unfamiliar multi-actor, distributed, operational, or leadership competencies the second project must independently prove.

competencies.statement: Map direct users, indirect users, operators, approvers, maintainers, adversaries, and affected non-users with their decisions and dependencies.
misconceptions: Only people who click the interface are product users.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map direct users, indirect users, operators, approvers, maintainers, adversaries, and affected non-users with their decisions and dependencies.

competencies.statement: Trace frontstage and backstage actions, handoffs, wait states, channels, policies, evidence, and breakdowns across an end-to-end service.
misconceptions: A screen-level user flow captures the whole service.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace frontstage and backstage actions, handoffs, wait states, channels, policies, evidence, and breakdowns across an end-to-end service.

competencies.statement: Expose conflicting speed, control, privacy, accessibility, workload, cost, and accountability outcomes instead of averaging them away.
misconceptions: One global success metric can represent every actor fairly.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Expose conflicting speed, control, privacy, accessibility, workload, cost, and accountability outcomes instead of averaging them away.

competencies.statement: Identify who may decide, approve, access, override, appeal, support, and answer for each consequential transition.
misconceptions: Administrative access implies legitimate authority for every action.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Identify who may decide, approve, access, override, appeal, support, and answer for each consequential transition.

competencies.statement: Judge when additional research is repeating known patterns, where evidence is still weak, and which groups remain missing.
misconceptions: A fixed interview count guarantees discovery saturation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Judge when additional research is repeating known patterns, where evidence is still weak, and which groups remain missing.

competencies.statement: Choose inform, consult, involve, collaborate, or shared-decision participation deliberately and communicate its actual influence.
misconceptions: Calling a session co-design grants participants decision power.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose inform, consult, involve, collaborate, or shared-decision participation deliberately and communicate its actual influence.

competencies.statement: Budget format, assistive technology, language, schedule, compensation, connectivity, cognitive load, and support accommodations before recruitment.
misconceptions: Offering a video call is sufficient access for all participants.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Budget format, assistive technology, language, schedule, compensation, connectivity, cognitive load, and support accommodations before recruitment.

competencies.statement: Compare benefits, burdens, error rates, exclusion, recourse, and resource demands across relevant groups without inventing demographic certainty.
misconceptions: Equal treatment necessarily produces equitable outcomes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare benefits, burdens, error rates, exclusion, recourse, and resource demands across relevant groups without inventing demographic certainty.

competencies.statement: Facilitate disagreement with explicit decision rules, psychological safety, evidence capture, dissent preservation, and escalation paths.
misconceptions: Consensus is the only legitimate co-design outcome.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Facilitate disagreement with explicit decision rules, psychological safety, evidence capture, dissent preservation, and escalation paths.

competencies.statement: Report what input changed, did not change, why, who decided, and how participants can challenge or withdraw.
misconceptions: Collecting feedback completes the obligation to participants.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Report what input changed, did not change, why, who decided, and how participants can challenge or withdraw.

competencies.statement: Define states, events, guards, actors, effects, notifications, terminal conditions, and invalid transitions in an executable table.
misconceptions: A list of status values defines workflow behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define states, events, guards, actors, effects, notifications, terminal conditions, and invalid transitions in an executable table.

competencies.statement: Bind each transition to authenticated role, contextual authorization, separation of duties, and appeal or override evidence.
misconceptions: Hiding a button prevents an unauthorized transition.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bind each transition to authenticated role, contextual authorization, separation of duties, and appeal or override evidence.

competencies.statement: Specify optimistic, pessimistic, serialized, or conflict-resolution behavior when actors act on stale state.
misconceptions: The last write is an acceptable default for every conflict.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Specify optimistic, pessimistic, serialized, or conflict-resolution behavior when actors act on stale state.

competencies.statement: Record append-only actor, authority, request, prior state, new state, reason, correlation, time, and outcome without leaking unnecessary data.
misconceptions: Application logs are automatically a reliable audit trail.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record append-only actor, authority, request, prior state, new state, reason, correlation, time, and outcome without leaking unnecessary data.

competencies.statement: Recover interrupted, duplicated, partially completed, or disputed transitions without fabricating history.
misconceptions: Retrying the whole workflow restores a consistent state.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Recover interrupted, duplicated, partially completed, or disputed transitions without fabricating history.

competencies.statement: Express accessibility, security, privacy, reliability, performance, modifiability, operability, and cost as stimulus-response-measure scenarios.
misconceptions: Words like scalable and secure are usable architecture requirements.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Express accessibility, security, privacy, reliability, performance, modifiability, operability, and cost as stimulus-response-measure scenarios.

competencies.statement: Compare modular monolith, service, event, batch, managed, and buy options against explicit quality scenarios and team constraints.
misconceptions: Microservices are the advanced default for a second project.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare modular monolith, service, event, batch, managed, and buy options against explicit quality scenarios and team constraints.

competencies.statement: Design runtime-validated APIs or events with compatibility, versioning, idempotency, pagination, error, deprecation, and consumer evidence.
misconceptions: Adding optional fields is always backward compatible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design runtime-validated APIs or events with compatibility, versioning, idempotency, pagination, error, deprecation, and consumer evidence.

competencies.statement: Enforce domain, application, adapter, and platform dependency direction with contract tests and replaceable boundaries.
misconceptions: Dependency injection alone creates correct architectural boundaries.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enforce domain, application, adapter, and platform dependency direction with contract tests and replaceable boundaries.

competencies.statement: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions.
misconceptions: An accepted architecture decision should not change during delivery.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Attach evidence, consequence, owner, expiry, and measurable review triggers to architecture decisions.

competencies.statement: Assign authoritative source, steward, identity, update rights, derived copies, synchronization, and dispute resolution for each entity.
misconceptions: The database containing a value owns its meaning.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assign authoritative source, steward, identity, update rights, derived copies, synchronization, and dispute resolution for each entity.

competencies.statement: Protect uniqueness, relationships, state invariants, transaction boundaries, and concurrency behavior at complementary layers.
misconceptions: Application validation prevents all invalid persisted state.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Protect uniqueness, relationships, state invariants, transaction boundaries, and concurrency behavior at complementary layers.

competencies.statement: Plan expand-migrate-contract changes with old/new reader and writer compatibility, backfill, verification, pause, and rollback.
misconceptions: A migration that succeeds once is safe for production data.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Plan expand-migrate-contract changes with old/new reader and writer compatibility, backfill, verification, pause, and rollback.

competencies.statement: Implement retention, legal or policy hold, export, correction, deletion, derived-data cleanup, and auditable exceptions.
misconceptions: Deleting the primary row removes every copy and derived artifact.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Implement retention, legal or policy hold, export, correction, deletion, derived-data cleanup, and auditable exceptions.

competencies.statement: Reconcile counts, checksums, invariants, samples, rejects, and actor-visible outcomes before and after migration or repair.
misconceptions: Matching total row counts proves a migration preserved meaning.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reconcile counts, checksums, invariants, samples, rejects, and actor-visible outcomes before and after migration or repair.

competencies.statement: Separate person, account, organization, service, device, credential, authenticator, session, and display identity.
misconceptions: One email address is a stable global person identity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate person, account, organization, service, device, credential, authenticator, session, and display identity.

competencies.statement: Choose authentication and step-up strength from threat, consequence, usability, accessibility, recovery, and platform evidence.
misconceptions: More authentication factors always produce a safer accessible system.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose authentication and step-up strength from threat, consequence, usability, accessibility, recovery, and platform evidence.

competencies.statement: Define issuance, binding, rotation, idle and absolute expiry, revocation, concurrent sessions, device evidence, and secure transport.
misconceptions: A signed token needs no server-side lifecycle policy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define issuance, binding, rotation, idle and absolute expiry, revocation, concurrent sessions, device evidence, and secure transport.

competencies.statement: Design recovery resistant to takeover and lockout with accessible alternatives, cooling, notification, audit, and support boundaries.
misconceptions: Security questions are a safe universal recovery method.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design recovery resistant to takeover and lockout with accessible alternatives, cooling, notification, audit, and support boundaries.

competencies.statement: Return usable authentication failures and telemetry without account enumeration, credential leakage, or inaccessible friction.
misconceptions: Generic errors alone prevent authentication information leaks.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Return usable authentication failures and telemetry without account enumeration, credential leakage, or inaccessible friction.

competencies.statement: Choose role, attribute, relationship, capability, or policy models from resource, action, subject, context, and review needs.
misconceptions: Role-based access control fits every authorization problem.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose role, attribute, relationship, capability, or policy models from resource, action, subject, context, and review needs.

competencies.statement: Enforce authorization at every trusted resource and transition boundary using current server-side state.
misconceptions: Client routing and hidden controls are meaningful authorization barriers.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enforce authorization at every trusted resource and transition boundary using current server-side state.

competencies.statement: Propagate tenant identity through query, cache, file, job, event, log, backup, restore, and support paths with adversarial tests.
misconceptions: Adding tenant_id to primary tables guarantees isolation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Propagate tenant identity through query, cache, file, job, event, log, backup, restore, and support paths with adversarial tests.

competencies.statement: Model automation, scraping, spam, enumeration, resource exhaustion, fraud, and moderator burden with proportional controls.
misconceptions: Rate limiting by IP solves automated abuse.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model automation, scraping, spam, enumeration, resource exhaustion, fraud, and moderator burden with proportional controls.

competencies.statement: Require least privilege, just-in-time or bounded elevation, reason, approval where needed, audit, notification, and revocation for privileged work.
misconceptions: Administrators should have unrestricted standing access.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Require least privilege, just-in-time or bounded elevation, reason, approval where needed, audit, notification, and revocation for privileged work.

competencies.statement: Assign stable operation, request, event, entity, and attempt identities so repeated delivery can be recognized and reconciled.
misconceptions: A timestamp is a sufficient idempotency key.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assign stable operation, request, event, entity, and attempt identities so repeated delivery can be recognized and reconciled.

competencies.statement: Define idempotency scope, result replay, conflict behavior, retention, and atomic relationship to the protected effect.
misconceptions: Retrying an idempotent HTTP method guarantees no duplicate business effect.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define idempotency scope, result replay, conflict behavior, retention, and atomic relationship to the protected effect.

competencies.statement: Distinguish rejected, failed, timed out, cancelled, partially completed, and unknown remote outcomes before retry or compensation.
misconceptions: A client timeout means the server performed no work.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish rejected, failed, timed out, cancelled, partially completed, and unknown remote outcomes before retry or compensation.

competencies.statement: State ordering, visibility, staleness, monotonicity, and conflict guarantees per workflow instead of claiming generic consistency.
misconceptions: Distributed events arrive exactly once and in creation order.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State ordering, visibility, staleness, monotonicity, and conflict guarantees per workflow instead of claiming generic consistency.

competencies.statement: Bound retries, backoff, concurrency, queues, circuit behavior, fallback, compensation, reconciliation, and operator recovery.
misconceptions: Automatic retries make an integration resilient.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound retries, backoff, concurrency, queues, circuit behavior, fallback, compensation, reconciliation, and operator recovery.

competencies.statement: Decompose work into outcome-bearing, low-coupling slices with owner, interface, acceptance, dependencies, and integration point.
misconceptions: Assigning files to people prevents collaboration conflicts.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Decompose work into outcome-bearing, low-coupling slices with owner, interface, acceptance, dependencies, and integration point.

competencies.statement: Link problem evidence, issue, decision, change, review, verification, release, and follow-up through stable identifiers.
misconceptions: A ticket title provides enough traceability.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Link problem evidence, issue, decision, change, review, verification, release, and follow-up through stable identifiers.

competencies.statement: Request review by risk area and require behavior, accessibility, security, data, operations, test, and documentation evidence.
misconceptions: Code style approval is a complete review.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Request review by risk area and require behavior, accessibility, security, data, operations, test, and documentation evidence.

competencies.statement: Surface disagreement, uncertainty, blockers, availability, and decision authority early with reversible escalation and handoff artifacts.
misconceptions: A high-performing team should resolve all disagreements informally.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Surface disagreement, uncertainty, blockers, availability, and decision authority early with reversible escalation and handoff artifacts.

competencies.statement: Maintain contribution, conduct, security, support, ownership, license, roadmap, release, and archival practices for project health.
misconceptions: Governance matters only after a project gains external contributors.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain contribution, conduct, security, support, ownership, license, roadmap, release, and archival practices for project health.

competencies.statement: Choose user-centered availability, correctness, latency, freshness, durability, or support indicators with valid measurement boundaries.
misconceptions: Server uptime directly measures user success.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose user-centered availability, correctness, latency, freshness, durability, or support indicators with valid measurement boundaries.

competencies.statement: Set objective, window, population, exclusions, error budget, and response policy from user need and operating capacity.
misconceptions: Every service should target 100 percent availability.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Set objective, window, population, exclusions, error budget, and response policy from user need and operating capacity.

competencies.statement: Correlate revision, request, actor class, workflow, dependency, event, log, metric, and trace without high-cardinality or privacy leakage.
misconceptions: More telemetry always improves diagnosis.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Correlate revision, request, actor class, workflow, dependency, event, log, metric, and trace without high-cardinality or privacy leakage.

competencies.statement: Provide discoverable accessible status, help, reporting, workarounds, response expectations, escalation, and communication during failure.
misconceptions: A public status page is sufficient user support.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Provide discoverable accessible status, help, reporting, workarounds, response expectations, escalation, and communication during failure.

competencies.statement: Preserve timeline and evidence, restore safely, communicate impact, analyze contributing conditions without blame, and track preventive work.
misconceptions: The person who made the triggering change is the incident root cause.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve timeline and evidence, restore safely, communicate impact, analyze contributing conditions without blame, and track preventive work.

competencies.statement: Set task, response, interaction, payload, memory, CPU, queue, accessibility, and energy budgets tied to user conditions.
misconceptions: One average latency target represents product performance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Set task, response, interaction, payload, memory, CPU, queue, accessibility, and energy budgets tied to user conditions.

competencies.statement: Model actors, operations, arrival patterns, concurrency, data sizes, locality, think time, bursts, failures, and growth.
misconceptions: Multiplying current traffic by ten is a realistic load model.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model actors, operations, arrival patterns, concurrency, data sizes, locality, think time, bursts, failures, and growth.

competencies.statement: Measure end-to-end and component behavior with controlled environments, percentiles, resource signals, variance, and profiler evidence.
misconceptions: The slowest-looking code is the system bottleneck.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure end-to-end and component behavior with controlled environments, percentiles, resource signals, variance, and profiler evidence.

competencies.statement: Bound queues and concurrency and define admission, degradation, prioritization, backpressure, shedding, retry, and recovery behavior.
misconceptions: Autoscaling prevents overload under every workload.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound queues and concurrency and define admission, degradation, prioritization, backpressure, shedding, retry, and recovery behavior.

competencies.statement: Attribute fixed and variable build, run, storage, transfer, support, accessibility, and failure costs to useful product outcomes.
misconceptions: Cheaper infrastructure automatically creates a more efficient product.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Attribute fixed and variable build, run, storage, transfer, support, accessibility, and failure costs to useful product outcomes.

competencies.statement: Bind reviewed source, protected workflow, isolated builder, inputs, dependencies, output digest, provenance, and verification.
misconceptions: A checksum proves who built an artifact and from which source.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bind reviewed source, protected workflow, isolated builder, inputs, dependencies, output digest, provenance, and verification.

competencies.statement: Maintain current direct, transitive, generated, container, service, data, model, and license inventory with vulnerability ownership.
misconceptions: A package lockfile is a complete SBOM.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain current direct, transitive, generated, container, service, data, model, and license inventory with vulnerability ownership.

competencies.statement: Constrain events, untrusted input, tokens, OIDC claims, secrets, runners, caches, environments, approvals, and audit evidence.
misconceptions: Private repositories make CI workflows trusted.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Constrain events, untrusted input, tokens, OIDC claims, secrets, runners, caches, environments, approvals, and audit evidence.

competencies.statement: Deploy the same immutable artifact through readiness, bounded exposure, user-centered signals, hold points, abort, and promotion.
misconceptions: A deployment that starts successfully is ready for full traffic.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Deploy the same immutable artifact through readiness, bounded exposure, user-centered signals, hold points, abort, and promotion.

competencies.statement: Rehearse compatible rollback, rollforward, configuration and data recovery, communication, and post-recovery verification.
misconceptions: Returning application code to an older version always restores the service.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Rehearse compatible rollback, rollforward, configuration and data recovery, communication, and post-recovery verification.

competencies.statement: Choose parallel, phased, cohort, shadow, strangler, import, or big-bang transition from reversibility, compatibility, user burden, and risk.
misconceptions: Big-bang migration is simplest because it avoids dual operation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose parallel, phased, cohort, shadow, strangler, import, or big-bang transition from reversibility, compatibility, user burden, and risk.

competencies.statement: Define recovery point and time needs, backup scope, integrity, encryption, access, retention, clean restore, and application reconciliation.
misconceptions: A successful backup job proves recoverability.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define recovery point and time needs, backup scope, integrity, encryption, access, retention, clean restore, and application reconciliation.

competencies.statement: Measure task outcomes, guardrails, adoption, abandonment, support, accessibility, reliability, and unintended effects over an appropriate period.
misconceptions: Launch-day feedback predicts sustained product value.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure task outcomes, guardrails, adoption, abandonment, support, accessibility, reliability, and unintended effects over an appropriate period.

competencies.statement: Defend personal contribution and shared outcomes using decisions, revisions, reviews, incidents, feedback, and changed-case evidence without claiming others work.
misconceptions: Commit count measures individual contribution.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend personal contribution and shared outcomes using decisions, revisions, reviews, incidents, feedback, and changed-case evidence without claiming others work.

competencies.statement: Evaluate product, process, leadership, ethics, operations, and learning against prior predictions and define a harder transfer challenge.
misconceptions: A retrospective is mainly a list of what went well and poorly.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate product, process, leadership, ethics, operations, and learning against prior predictions and define a harder transfer challenge.

credential.title: Independent Product Studio II: Multi-Actor Systems, Delivery, and Operations Certificate

modules.title: Prior-Product Audit, Maintenance Reality, and Reframed Challenge
modules.description: A successful first project has dependency drift, unresolved feedback, stale assumptions, hidden operations work, and a proposed sequel based only on more features. Learners cumulatively produce maintenance audit and reframed product charter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Audit which prior claims survived real use, changed data, dependency drift, support, accessibility, security, and recovery conditions.
Classify feedback by user outcome, evidence source, frequency, severity, reach, confidence, and strategic fit.
Measure dependency, defect, support, content, data, infrastructure, and cognitive maintenance cost before adding scope.

modules.title: Multi-Actor Service Discovery and Conflicting Outcomes
modules.description: Operators, end users, administrators, support staff, and affected non-users have different workflows, incentives, harms, and definitions of success. Learners cumulatively produce multi-actor service map and conflict register through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Map direct users, indirect users, operators, approvers, maintainers, adversaries, and affected non-users with their decisions and dependencies.
Trace frontstage and backstage actions, handoffs, wait states, channels, policies, evidence, and breakdowns across an end-to-end service.
Expose conflicting speed, control, privacy, accessibility, workload, cost, and accountability outcomes instead of averaging them away.

modules.title: Co-Design, Equity, and Accessible Participation
modules.description: The team invites affected people only after scope is fixed and treats feedback as approval rather than shared problem framing and decision power. Learners cumulatively produce co-design plan and equity impact record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose inform, consult, involve, collaborate, or shared-decision participation deliberately and communicate its actual influence.
Budget format, assistive technology, language, schedule, compensation, connectivity, cognitive load, and support accommodations before recruitment.
Compare benefits, burdens, error rates, exclusion, recourse, and resource demands across relevant groups without inventing demographic certainty.

modules.title: Multi-Actor Workflow, State Machines, and Auditability
modules.description: Concurrent actors can approve, cancel, retry, reopen, and override work, but the product models only a mutable status string and timestamps. Learners cumulatively produce authorized auditable workflow model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define states, events, guards, actors, effects, notifications, terminal conditions, and invalid transitions in an executable table.
Bind each transition to authenticated role, contextual authorization, separation of duties, and appeal or override evidence.
Specify optimistic, pessimistic, serialized, or conflict-resolution behavior when actors act on stale state.

modules.title: Quality-Attribute Architecture, ADRs, and Evolvable Interfaces
modules.description: Teams split a product into services before measuring coupling, deployment need, failure boundaries, team ownership, or compatibility cost. Learners cumulatively produce quality-attribute architecture and interface suite through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Express accessibility, security, privacy, reliability, performance, modifiability, operability, and cost as stimulus-response-measure scenarios.
Compare modular monolith, service, event, batch, managed, and buy options against explicit quality scenarios and team constraints.
Design runtime-validated APIs or events with compatibility, versioning, idempotency, pagination, error, deprecation, and consumer evidence.

modules.title: Data Ownership, Integrity, Migrations, and Lifecycle Governance
modules.description: Shared records have unclear ownership, incompatible meanings, uncontrolled copies, irreversible migrations, and no correction or deletion path. Learners cumulatively produce governed data model and migration rehearsal through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Assign authoritative source, steward, identity, update rights, derived copies, synchronization, and dispute resolution for each entity.
Protect uniqueness, relationships, state invariants, transaction boundaries, and concurrency behavior at complementary layers.
Plan expand-migrate-contract changes with old/new reader and writer compatibility, backfill, verification, pause, and rollback.

modules.title: Identity, Authentication, Sessions, and Recovery
modules.description: The product conflates account, person, credential, role, session, and tenant while password reset and support override bypass normal protections. Learners cumulatively produce identity and session assurance model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Separate person, account, organization, service, device, credential, authenticator, session, and display identity.
Choose authentication and step-up strength from threat, consequence, usability, accessibility, recovery, and platform evidence.
Define issuance, binding, rotation, idle and absolute expiry, revocation, concurrent sessions, device evidence, and secure transport.

modules.title: Authorization, Tenant Isolation, and Abuse Resistance
modules.description: Authenticated users can change identifiers, cross tenant boundaries, automate expensive actions, and exploit support or administrator workflows. Learners cumulatively produce deny-by-default authorization and abuse case suite through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose role, attribute, relationship, capability, or policy models from resource, action, subject, context, and review needs.
Enforce authorization at every trusted resource and transition boundary using current server-side state.
Propagate tenant identity through query, cache, file, job, event, log, backup, restore, and support paths with adversarial tests.

modules.title: Concurrency, Idempotency, Partial Failure, and Resilient Integration
modules.description: Retries, duplicate events, reordered messages, timeouts, and dependency outages create duplicate effects and contradictory state. Learners cumulatively produce fault-aware integration and recovery harness through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Assign stable operation, request, event, entity, and attempt identities so repeated delivery can be recognized and reconciled.
Define idempotency scope, result replay, conflict behavior, retention, and atomic relationship to the protected effect.
Distinguish rejected, failed, timed out, cancelled, partially completed, and unknown remote outcomes before retry or compensation.

modules.title: Team Collaboration, Review, Governance, and Decision Handoffs
modules.description: Parallel contributors create hidden work, oversized changes, ambiguous ownership, rubber-stamp review, and undocumented decisions. Learners cumulatively produce collaboration operating system and review evidence through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Decompose work into outcome-bearing, low-coupling slices with owner, interface, acceptance, dependencies, and integration point.
Link problem evidence, issue, decision, change, review, verification, release, and follow-up through stable identifiers.
Request review by risk area and require behavior, accessibility, security, data, operations, test, and documentation evidence.

modules.title: Observability, Service Levels, Support, and Incident Learning
modules.description: The product emits many logs but cannot answer whether users succeed, which revision failed, who is affected, or how to restore service. Learners cumulatively produce user-centered operations and incident system through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose user-centered availability, correctness, latency, freshness, durability, or support indicators with valid measurement boundaries.
Set objective, window, population, exclusions, error budget, and response policy from user need and operating capacity.
Correlate revision, request, actor class, workflow, dependency, event, log, metric, and trace without high-cardinality or privacy leakage.

modules.title: Performance, Capacity, Efficiency, and Cost Tradeoffs
modules.description: The team optimizes synthetic speed while real users face slow devices, large payloads, queue growth, resource contention, and unpredictable bills. Learners cumulatively produce capacity and cost evidence model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Set task, response, interaction, payload, memory, CPU, queue, accessibility, and energy budgets tied to user conditions.
Model actors, operations, arrival patterns, concurrency, data sizes, locality, think time, bursts, failures, and growth.
Measure end-to-end and component behavior with controlled environments, percentiles, resource signals, variance, and profiler evidence.

modules.title: Trusted Delivery, Supply Chain, Progressive Release, and Rollback
modules.description: A release rebuilds across environments, uses mutable dependencies, grants broad automation authority, and has no provenance, canary, or rollback rehearsal. Learners cumulatively produce trusted progressive delivery dossier through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Bind reviewed source, protected workflow, isolated builder, inputs, dependencies, output digest, provenance, and verification.
Maintain current direct, transitive, generated, container, service, data, model, and license inventory with vulnerability ownership.
Constrain events, untrusted input, tokens, OIDC claims, secrets, runners, caches, environments, approvals, and audit evidence.

modules.title: Migration, Recovery, Longitudinal Validation, and Advanced Defense
modules.description: The team plans a big-bang cutover, one launch-day usability test, an untested backup, and a demo that hides operating and collaboration evidence. Learners cumulatively produce advanced product transition and defense packet through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose parallel, phased, cohort, shadow, strangler, import, or big-bang transition from reversibility, compatibility, user burden, and risk.
Define recovery point and time needs, backup scope, integrity, encryption, access, retention, clean restore, and application reconciliation.
Measure task outcomes, guardrails, adoption, abandonment, support, accessibility, reliability, and unintended effects over an appropriate period.
